programmes.show_description: 1/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors share their favourite UK psychedelic music. Contains language which some may find offensive.
2/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors share records which have shaped their career.
3/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors celebrate women in post-punk.
4/4. 6 Music Festival 2026 headliners The Horrors share some of their favourite new artists.
Emily Pilbeam presents a mixtape of her personal selection of tracks from BBC Introducing, with highlights and conversation from the BBC Introducing night at the 6 Music Festival.
It's Amy Lamé in for Chris Hawkins with a stack of great tunes for your Monday morning.
Deb Grant sits in with wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Broadcaster and writer Zakia tells us about her new book Finding Albion, which is a deep dive into the nation's long-forgotten culture and traditions.
Chris shares some of his highlights from the 6 Music Festival 2026, with live music from Naima Bock and Courtney Barnett.
Steve has brand new music from Lambrini Girls and Liz Lawrence and repeats some of Bloc Party's set from the 6 Music Festival in Salford.
Nathan and Deb unveil this week's Breaking Artist, Philadelphia group Full Body 2, following the release of their single Turn So Slow.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
1/4. Kim Gordon shares some of her favourites songs, and some of the artists that inspired Sonic Youth.
Massive Attack live and in conversation at the BBC. Featuring archive 6 Music material and interviews with Matt Everitt, Julie Cullen, Stuart Maconie & Mark Radcliffe.
Massive Attack recorded live in concert. Featuring performances from Glastonbury and the Royal Albert Hall.
Colleen 'Cosmo' Murphy, founder of Classic Album Sundays, argues that Blue Lines by Massive Attack is the definitive album from Bristol, in the fifth episode of 'Sounds of a City'.

programmes.show_name: The First Time with Massive Attack's 3D
programmes.show_description: Massive Attack's 3D shares his musical milestones, in conversation with Matt Everitt. From early sound systems in Bristol to stellar collaborations, via the peak years of Trip-Hop.
Featuring selections from their key albums, dub remixes and collaborators Mad Professor & Horace Andy among others.
Amy Lamé kick-starts your day with the best new music and forgotten classics. Plus, your chance to vote on a Lost Love.
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
Arlo Parks live in session from Maida Vale, playing tracks from her latest album, Ambiguous Desire.
Chris chats to Clari from 'angry weepy chaos rok trio' Mary In The Junkyard about their new music, and we take a moment to chill out in the Zen Den.
Professor Sophie Daniels joins Huw for a special 6 Musicology on Pulp, and Alexis Taylor tells Huw the 'Song He wishes he'd written'.
Sets from R.AGGS and Naima Bock recorded live at Eccles Town Hall for 6 Music Festival.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
2/4. Kim Gordon shares some of her favourites songs, and some of the artists that inspired Sonic Youth. Tonight's show includes music from Neil Young and Oliver Nelson.
